candidate_summary: JHATOR-EXTERNAL-JUDGE: multi-stage external-classifier-pool acceptance criterion modelled on Tibetan jhator sky burial. Diverse vulture-judge pool with N-of-N full-consumption consensus criterion + multi-stage re-grind rewrite-resubmit iteration (k <= accepts < N triggers re-grind; up to R re-offer rounds before absolute reject).

results.name: Multi-Agent Debate LLM Judges Adaptive Stability Detection (2510.12697)
results.description: LLM-side: multi-agent debate judge with stability detection via time-varying Beta-Binomial mixture + Kolmogorov-Smirnov stopping; improves over majority voting.
keyword_matches: external multi-judge LLM acceptance criterion (partial: 'judge' + 'LLM' present; loose substring of 'multi-judge')
results.rationale: Multi-agent debate judge pool with adaptive stopping is the same artefact (diverse external LLM judge ensemble) producing same effect (multi-judge consensus acceptance) via a different mechanism (Beta-Binomial KS-stopping vs N-of-N full-consumption + re-grind). Meets v5 0.7 same-artefact same-effect bar.

results.name: Multi-Agent LLM Judge Automatic Personalized Design (2504.02867)
results.description: LLM-side: iterative multi-agent prompt-refinement system with ReWrite agent + judge + feedback loop.
keyword_matches: external multi-judge LLM acceptance criterion (judge + LLM in title; multi-agent)
multi-stage re-grind rewrite-resubmit loop (ReWrite agent + feedback loop in snippet)
results.rationale: ReWrite agent + judge + iterative feedback loop is essentially identical artefact and effect to JHATOR's re-grind rewrite-resubmit + judge-pool acceptance. Different mechanism (prompt refinement personalisation vs full-consumption consensus) but core artefact and effect match.

results.name: Agent-as-a-Judge Evaluation LLMs (2508.02994)
results.description: LLM-side: comprehensive review of agent-as-a-judge evaluation paradigm — diverse agent verifier pools.
keyword_matches: external multi-judge LLM acceptance criterion (judge + LLM + evaluation)
vulture-pool consensus diversity judge ('diverse agent verifier pools' matches diversity + judge/verifier pool)
results.rationale: Agent-as-a-judge survey explicitly covers diverse agent verifier pools (same artefact as JHATOR vulture-judge pool) producing evaluation/acceptance decisions (same effect). Mechanism differs (general paradigm survey vs specific full-consumption + re-grind) but meets 0.7 same-artefact same-effect bar.

results.name: LLM Meta-Judges Multi-Agent Framework (2504.17087)
results.description: LLM-side: meta-judges for collaborative decision-making — agents share judgments and update on aggregate.
keyword_matches: external multi-judge LLM acceptance criterion (meta-judges + LLM + multi-agent)
vulture-pool consensus diversity judge (agents share judgments + aggregate consensus)
results.rationale: Meta-judge multi-agent share-and-update on aggregate is same artefact (judge pool) producing same effect (consensus acceptance) via different mechanism (meta-aggregation vs full-consumption N-of-N + re-grind). Meets v5 0.7.

results.name: Self-Refine Iterative Refinement Self-Feedback (2303.17651)
results.description: LLM-side: iterative FEEDBACK→REFINE→FEEDBACK loop with same LLM as feedback + refinement.
keyword_matches: multi-stage re-grind rewrite-resubmit loop ('iterative ... loop' + refine = rewrite-resubmit)
results.rationale: Self-Refine FEEDBACK->REFINE loop is the same artefact (iterative critique+refine pipeline) and same effect (rewrite-resubmit until acceptable) as JHATOR re-grind loop, but at single-agent (self) granularity rather than external diverse pool. Meets 0.7 same-artefact same-effect with different mechanism.

results.name: Self-Reflection LLM Academic Response 2025 npj AI
results.description: LLM-side: self-reflection enhances response quality via accept-reject critique loop.
keyword_matches: multi-stage re-grind rewrite-resubmit loop ('accept-reject critique loop' = iterative resubmit; loop substring)
results.rationale: Self-reflection accept-reject critique loop covers the iterative acceptance-criterion paradigm (same effect: iterative accept-reject driving rewrite). Single-agent self-reflection rather than external multi-judge pool — artefact partially differs. Just meets 0.7 functional threshold.

results.name: HalluLens LLM Hallucination Benchmark (2504.17550)
results.description: LLM-side: external benchmark for hallucination acceptance criterion.
keyword_matches: external multi-judge LLM acceptance criterion ('external' + 'LLM' + 'acceptance criterion' substring match)
results.rationale: HalluLens is a benchmark (static evaluation dataset), not a multi-stage iterative re-grind judge-pool protocol. Same domain (external acceptance for LLM output) but different artefact (benchmark vs runtime judge pool with rewrite-resubmit) and weaker effect overlap. Candidate's own novelty claim explicitly differentiates from HalluLens. Below 0.7 on all axes.

results.name: Survey Hallucination LLM Detection Mitigation 2025
results.description: LLM-side: catalog of external-classifier hallucination detection methods including ensemble + critique-refine.
keyword_matches: external scavenger natural classifier ('external-classifier' substring matches 'external' + 'classifier')
results.rationale: Survey explicitly catalogs external-classifier ensemble + critique-refine methods, which functionally saturates the JHATOR contribution space (diverse pool + iterative critique-refine = re-grind). Same artefact category and same effect catalogued; different mechanism granularity (catalog vs specific full-consumption rule). Two content-word substrings match (external-classifier).

decision_rationale: Independent re-judging confirms 7 of 8 results clear at least one hit axis. Two near-exact functional twins (2504.02867 ReWrite+judge iterative loop = re-grind rewrite-resubmit; 2510.12697 multi-agent debate judge with stability stopping = diverse vulture-pool consensus + stopping rule) saturate JHATOR's core mechanism. Agent-as-a-Judge (2508.02994) explicitly covers diverse verifier pools; Meta-Judges (2504.17087) covers aggregate consensus; Self-Refine (2303.17651) covers iterative feedback->refine loop. Recombination of well-mainstream components (multi-judge pool + iterative rewrite-resubmit) is functionally pre-empted across multiple distinct effect-clusters. Only HalluLens (id 7) fails all three axes; the other seven hit. Total hits >=2 with functional-twin saturation -> FAIL.
